prompt:
Our board-games club wants to referee its draughts ladder by email, so I need an engine that's utterly strict about the rules and utterly predictable in how it lists moves — two runs on two laptops must agree token for token. Go module go-checkers, package checkers at the root, stdlib only. English draughts (checkers), 8x8.

Board and notation: squares are file a-h (left to right) and rank 1-8; the fixture/render format shows rank 8 as the FIRST line. Only dark squares are playable — those where file index (a=0) plus rank number is odd, so a1, c1, b2, ... Pieces: 'w'/'b' men, 'W'/'B' kings, '.' empty. Load(fixture string) (*Game, error) reads a 'turn: b' or 'turn: w' line followed by the 8 board lines (blank lines skipped) and rejects anything malformed: wrong line count/length, unknown characters, a piece on a light square, or an uncrowned man sitting on his own crowning rank. Render() gives back exactly the 8 board lines joined with \n (no turn line, no trailing newline); Turn() returns 'b' or 'w'.

Rules: white men step diagonally toward rank 8, black men toward rank 1, kings both ways. Captures jump an adjacent enemy onto the empty square beyond, the same piece keeps jumping as long as another jump exists (each enemy jumped at most once), and if ANY capture is available to the mover then quiet moves are illegal — a capture chain also can't be cut short. A man reaching his crowning rank (white 8, black 1) becomes a king and the move ends ON THE SPOT, even mid-chain with jumps seemingly still open. Moves are written 'c3-d4' for steps and 'c3xe5xc7' for full jump chains.

LegalMoves() []string must come out in one exact order: scan origin squares from rank 8 down to rank 1, file a to h within a rank; from each piece try directions up-left, up-right, down-left, down-right (up = toward rank 8, left = toward file a), skipping directions the piece can't use; jump chains are emitted depth-first extending in that same direction order. Apply(move string) error accepts a move only if it is string-equal to one of LegalMoves() (illegal or malformed input errors out and changes nothing), performs it — removing every jumped piece, crowning if earned — and flips the turn. Winner() (byte, bool): the game ends when the side to move has no legal move (wiped out or completely blocked); that side loses.

Transcript(fixture string, moves []string) (string, error) replays a game for the ladder archive: a 'start' block with the initial render, then per move a 'move N (b): b6xd4' header plus the render after it, blocks separated by blank lines, closing with 'winner: w' or 'next: b', every line \n-terminated. Any bad fixture or illegal move aborts with an error.

testdata/*.brd holds club positions; checkers_test.go pins opening enumerations, forced-capture lists, a branching double-jump, kinging cutting a chain short, king move order, blocked-side losses and two full transcripts. go test -race -timeout 30s ./... must pass — tests and fixtures are read-only.
